beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check weather and work route for SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: Wyatt wakes early on a rainy Tuesday; the SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon will affect his Renton commute, so he's mentally prepping an alternate route (I-90 via Bellevue will add ~20 min). Yesterday's moderate energy (50/100) and quiet evening letter-writing set a calm, deliberate tone for today.

beats.activity: Drive to work via I-90 south to avoid SR-520 closure; rain and heavier traffic.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Wyatt leaves early enough to avoid peak closure time but takes I-90 detour, adding ~15 min to his usual route. The steady rain from 7am–2pm means slower driving and wet conditions.

beats.activity: Warehouse logistics work: inventory coordination, shipping manifests, afternoon run planning.
beats.reasoning: Standard morning-into-early-afternoon shift. The rain and bridge closure create mild operational friction, but Wyatt's role is desk-based coordination, so he stays focused on scheduling and inventory flow.

beats.activity: Grab a quick lunch (sandwich, coffee) at desk or nearby break room.
beats.reasoning: Echoes yesterday's desk sandwich habit. Rain and grey mood push him to stay indoors; no errand for food outside the warehouse.

beats.activity: Afternoon warehouse work: shipping coordination, route finalization, end-of-day inventory checks.
beats.reasoning: Continues standard warehouse shift. By mid-afternoon, the rain is still steady (until 2pm) but clearing slightly. Wyatt stays focused on closing out the day's logistics.

beats.activity: Drive home from Renton via I-90; rain tapering off, evening light returning.
beats.reasoning: Standard return commute. Rain has ended by now (stopped at 2pm); slightly longer than usual due to residual congestion from the morning closure, but Wyatt is not stressed—he leaves at a calm, deliberate pace.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner; change into comfortable clothes.
beats.reasoning: Early evening meal at home. Wyatt is introverted and settled; no big social plans tonight. He's home early enough to relax before evening leisure.

beats.activity: Board game night with close friends at home; low-key, familiar crowd.
beats.reasoning: Wyatt values time with close friends over crowds. A low-key board game evening at home fits his introverted personality and yesterday's reflective mood. He hosts or joins a familiar group—no pressure, just good company and a game or two.

beats.activity: Wind down: friends depart, write a letter or read; prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: After friends leave, Wyatt has quiet time to himself—maybe another handwritten letter or a short read. This aligns with yesterday's letter-writing mood and sets up a calm transition to sleep.
Solid 7h 45m sleep. Wyatt is a creature of habit; early bedtime after a social but low-stress day. Overnight span clearly visible on the map.

diary: Woke to that grey drizzle and immediately checked the SR-520 closure—knew I'd lose an hour if I wasn't careful. Took I-90 south instead, crawling through the rain with everyone else who had the same idea. The warehouse was its usual Tuesday self: manifests, inventory counts, the quiet hum of the place. Grabbed a sandwich at my desk around eleven and kept moving through the afternoon. The rain was mostly done by the time I drove home, and the light came back enough to make the drive feel less oppressive. Made a decent dinner, changed into my old sweats, and then the crew showed up for board games. We played three rounds of something nobody quite remembered the rules for, which somehow made it funnier. By the time they left around six, I was ready for the quiet again. Just me, a cup of tea, and the sound of the rain stopping.
thought_process: The SR-520 closure threw me off at first—that's my normal route—but I caught it early enough in the coffee fog to plan around it. The detour via I-90 added time, sure, but it wasn't worth the stress of fighting the emergency repair crowds. Work was steady, unremarkable, which is how I prefer Tuesdays. I kept my head down, moved through the tasks, didn't overthink it. The rain actually helped my mood—something about grey days makes the day feel legitimate, less like I'm supposed to be somewhere else. By evening I was genuinely ready for the board game crowd; I'd been alone in my head most of the day, so the noise and laughter felt right. Nothing dramatic, just the weight of a normal day lifting.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
